title: Hattie the Mouse and the Backyard Cleanup
summary: Hattie the mouse discovers that her beautiful backyard is overflowing with an assortment of junk just as the spring blossoms begin to appear. Expecting a visit from her Nana, Peter Rabbit, Hattie decides it is time to tidy up. When Nana arrives on Sunday, the two survey the mess—which includes everything from old electronics and toys to bricks and even "boogies." Armed with bags and shovels, Hattie and Nana Peter Rabbit work together to scoop up the clutter and haul it all to the trash, leaving the yard pristine and ready for the season.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Do you want to jump in the chair? Okay, here you go.

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: Yes!

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Okay, ready for Stars and Stories?

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: Yes! On my blanket, right?

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Right.

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: You put on my blanket, daddy, or otherwise I'll be cold without a blanket. So I'll put on a long sleeve shirt.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Oh, okay. Right now?

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: Hoo, hoo, hoo!

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Okay. Do you want this one? Oh, this is a short sleeve shirt.

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: No short sleeve shirt! That, I'll be cold! I don't want it, no thank you.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Okay, well how about a long sleeve shirt?

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: A long sleeve shirt, right! Right now, right now. We still have stars, we still have stars, we still have stars, we still have stars, we still have stars.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: We still have stars. Would you like this one?

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: Yes! But I don't want it. I want something, but not that one, no, no.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: We only have so many options here. How about...?

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: No! I won't pick any of those.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Alright, we're gonna have to wait to choose a shirt then if you won't pick any of those.

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: But I want to!

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Or how about this one?

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: No! Or what others do you have? Wait, what others? What other? No. Yes, yes, I do!

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Oh, what made you pick this one?

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: Because it's funny!

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: What's funny about it?

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: It has chickens and snowflakes. But why does it have snowflakes?

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: That, I do not know.

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: But I think it's winter.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: It's a winter chicken shirt. That's interesting, isn't it?

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: Winter chicken shirt.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Winter chicken. Are you a winter chicken?

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: No! But soon I'll be. I'm a winter chicken dada, but now I'm not.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Oh, sorry. Let me just get the sleeve...

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: Daddy, can you roll up the sleeves?

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Yeah, let me just help you get the other sleeve on.

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: Roll up the sleeves, roll up the sleeves!

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Alright, I'm doing it.

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: But right now I want it like this.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: There, okay. A little bit less rolled up, like that. All right, now, let's check the time. Okay, we still have time for a short Stars and Stories.

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: Blanket, a blanket on me, on me, I'm in him, I'm in him.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: All right, now we're back to the stars. Okay, who should tonight's Stars and Stories be about?

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: Hattie, Izzy Bell Hattie.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: And what is Hattie going to do in tonight's story?

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: She tells a story and then you find out.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Once upon a time, Hattie the mouse was telling a story at bedtime. Who was she telling her story to?

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: No, she tells a story and then finally you'll find out.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Oh, you don't want Hattie to be telling a story in the story? Okay. Once upon a time, Hattie the mouse was in her big beautiful backyard behind her...

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: Workshop!

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: She was in her workshop and she opened the back door and she thought, 'Gosh, spring has sprung and it's so beautiful out and everything's blossoming, but my backyard is full of junk. What am I going to do about this?'

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: Maybe you can wait until your nana come, but Peter Rabbit is going to come too. That's your nana. Peter Rabbit, your nana, is going to come on Sunday.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Oh, yes. Hattie the mouse said to herself, 'Peter Rabbit, my nana, is coming on Sunday.' And so she waited until Sunday and then guess who showed up at her door? Peter Rabbit, her nana. And what did Peter Rabbit say?

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: Is your rug dirty or is outside have junk?

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Well, said Hattie the mouse, good question. My backyard is full of junk. Let's go look at it together. And what did Peter Rabbit notice first?

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: There was all junk.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Like what kind of junk?

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: Boogies!

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Boogies?

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: Yeah, boogies!

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: And Hattie said, 'Oh my gosh, boogies! I had no idea I had boogies in my backyard. How do you think we should clean them up?'

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: Diaper pillows, diaper bottles, milk, aqua, flowers, stickers, plastic metal, robot, electronics, LEDs, grass, soap suds, water, toys, and dirt and plants and flowers and birds and all kinds of things.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: And Hattie said, 'Yes, I suppose all those things are in my backyard and a lot of those things are junk. So let's...'

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: Bricks.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Bricks, yes. Let's pick one way to clean them up. What tools should we use to clean up the junk, Peter Rabbit?

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: Bags! Or shovels and you can scoop up the little pieces of the junk and put them into your bag and poof!

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Oh, okay, Hattie mouse said. Well, I'll get one bag and here's a bag for you, Peter Rabbit Nana. Now let's clean up the junk. And Hattie...

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: No the junk.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: The junk. And Hattie the mouse scooped up some of this and scooped up some of that and scooped up a little of everything. All the different kinds of junk went into her bag and Peter Rabbit did it too. And Hattie said, 'Now what? Where do we put these bags?'

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: We just put them in the trash.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: We just put them in the trash. And then Hattie looked around at her yard and she said, 'Wow, we cleaned up so much junk. It seems so much cleaner. Thanks for coming and helping, Nana Peter Rabbit.' And what did Peter Rabbit Nana say?

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: Thank you!

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: All right, and that's the end of our story. So we'll turn on the light and we'll turn out the stars.

characters.name: Hattie the Mouse
characters.description: A nimble, friendly mouse of Possible Street and loyal friend to Izzy and Seeker, this brave artisan is the wife of Dimitar and mistress of the Workshop of Hats—a weathered hollow tree sanctuary on Cornell Street scented with cedar, ink, and peanut butter toast. Clad in her signature red cloak and pointy hat, she is a resourceful hatmaker whose spirit was forged against massive diggers and the rain-slicked Floofer Cloud. Within her paint-stained home, she carefully curates her husband’s library alongside her own hand-painted volumes while confronting a baffling supply mystery; the discovery of curious clover stickers signals a gathering shadow of intrigue she is determined to unravel.

characters.name: Peter Rabbit
characters.description: Every Sunday, Hattie’s Nana—a diligent bunny with a mechanical mind—arrives to spearhead ambitious backyard cleaning projects. Whether she is hauling brush or recalibrating a temperamental stove with her steady paws, her tireless spirit and technical handiness make her an indispensable force of domestic restoration.

places.name: Backyard
places.description: Behind Hattie’s workshop and Uncle Griscko’s house, an eclectic sprawl of junk litters a landscape once home to a lush kelp forest, long since devoured by a mysterious shadow. A towering spaceship now looms over the site’s inaugural breach—a raw, jagged excavation where the earth was first struck and the bitten pool noodle was originally unearthed.

places.name: Workshop
places.description: Tucked at the edge of her sprawling backyard, Hattie’s home and creative sanctuary are nestled within the weathered heart of a hollow tree stump. This cozy, sun-dappled workspace blends domestic warmth with artistic industry, creating an evocative haven where the mouse brings her visions to life amidst the scent of aged bark and wild clover.
